title: DAO processing guards disjoint + multi-target extraction three-way (same DAO bytes install different edges)
description: Guards: Rust checks lollipop DAOSequence replay per target (routing.rs:351-357) but NO rpl_instance_id/dodag_id check; C does NO sequence check (replays accepted) but rejects mismatched instance/DODAG ID (routing.c:583-586); Python has NEITHER (dao.py:123-137). A replayed stale DAO: rejected by Rust, accepted by C and Python. A foreign-DODAG DAO: rejected by C, accepted by Rust and Python. Multi-target extraction: Python _extract_edge (dao.py:166-180) raises DaoError (rejects entire DAO); Rust extract_edge (routing.rs:363-382): LAST target wins, LAST transit wins, any option order; C extract_edge (routing.c:382-411): FIRST Target->Transit pair wins, requires target to precede transit. Same DAO bytes install three different parent edges. New aspect beyond tracked 2auf.44.3/.5. Fix: define the guard set (both checks) and multi-target semantics in the spec, then align all three.
